items.fix: Run tsc (e.g. tsc --noEmit) separately in CI.
items.why: Fast transpilers strip types without checking them; type errors slip through.
items.example: Vite happily ships `const n: number = 'oops'` because esbuild only erases types. CI: `tsc --noEmit` catches it before deploy.

items.mistake: Using import * as X to grab one helper.
items.fix: Import the named exports you need.
items.why: Namespace and dynamic imports can defeat tree-shaking and bloat the bundle.
items.example:
```ts
// BAD: drags every util into the bundle
import * as utils from './utils'
utils.cap('hi')

// GOOD: only 'cap' ends up in the bundle
import { cap } from './utils'
cap('hi')
```

items.mistake: Bundling secrets via VITE_/build-time env into client code.
items.fix: Keep secrets server-side; only expose truly public values to the client.
items.why: Anything bundled into the frontend is visible to users.
items.example: Vite inlines `import.meta.env.VITE_OPENAI_KEY` into the JS bundle — visible via DevTools. Move the call to a server route and read `process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY` there.

items.mistake: Hand-rolling complex webpack config when a default would do.
items.fix: Start with Vite (or a framework default) and customize only as needed.
items.why: Avoids unnecessary configuration burden and bugs.
items.example: A 300-line `webpack.config.js` to do what `npm create vite@latest` does out of the box: TS, HMR, code splitting, env handling, prod minification. Start with the default.
